title: Introductory Meditations
location: Introductory Meditations
text: Five thousand years ago, the “ Thrice Greatest Hermes ” said : “O Egypt! Egypt! There shall remain for thee for future generations only fables that no one will believe. Nothing of thee shall endure except words cut out in stone.” On the moonéd plains of Chaldea, and by the dark waters of Hgypt, thousands of years before the dawn of our own era, the most illustrious philosophers and great prophets of that time—the very soul of Egypt— sought through the development of the inner sight, in solitude, fasting, and by the intense revery which brings tranquillity and peacefulness of mind, to probe the secrets of nature, and so unroll the dark veil of mystery that shrouds eternity, and the reason of man’s existence. They became conscious that there is a golden sun of the intelligence, that can unfold the imagination, but not the imagination that is merely fancy. For fancy is foolishness, and the two are often confounded by the ignorant. Through this power of the spirit their consciousness grew away from its fleshly home to its starry habitation. An Arabian Astrologer said : “The soul sleepeth not, nor wanteth sleep, even as the stars, to which there is also a soul, wherewith an intent passion of our own doth make an union.” This glorious faculty of the intellect,

title: Introductory Meditations
location: Introductory Meditations
text: An Arabian Astrologer said : “The soul sleepeth not, nor wanteth sleep, even as the stars, to which there is also a soul, wherewith an intent passion of our own doth make an union.” This glorious faculty of the intellect, imagination, is the fire of the spirit—formless, cold as ice, and crystal clear. That mighty initiator of Egypt, Hermes, said : “There are two powers in the human mind— Fire and Earth, Imagination and Reason-—that suffice for the operation of all that is wonderful and glorious.” Paracelsus says : “ There is truth in Astrology, because it is nature, but it is not the final word, as there is a Spirit greater than nature.” To be conscious of that Divine Spirit one must feel the mystery of nature, for the soul riseth as one contemplates its majesty and beauty. All life is bound together by a common link, a mysterious affinity. It gives to us the understanding that there is a Universal Spirit distinct from the things themselves, a centre from which all radiates, an eternal fount of all life; it gives a blessing and a power, for every one is a part of a vast though unseen essence, that mystery of life—Spirit. Hermes says : “Listen within yourselves and look into the infinitude of Space and Time.” Space wherein

title: Introductory Meditations
location: Introductory Meditations
text: it gives a blessing and a power, for every one is a part of a vast though unseen essence, that mystery of life—Spirit. Hermes says : “Listen within yourselves and look into the infinitude of Space and Time.” Space wherein are other solar systems, and limitless worlds, so illimitable and infinite is the cosmos.

title: Introductory Meditations
location: Introductory Meditations
text: The Ancient Egyptians represented Time by a circle, illustrated as a serpent biting its own tail, meaning the eternal circle of things ; and sometimes it was made in the likeness of the figure 8, having neither beginning nor end. The twenty-four hours which comprise day and night we know as time, relative to this terrestrial planet, the earth. Astronomers understand and use a different calculation, in which they measure the duration of the movements of the Sun, Moon and Stars. This is called “‘ Sidereal Time,” and in relation to our own is twenty-four hours in a year, or four minutes in a day. A hundred years on the earth is but a hundred days in “ Sidereal Time.” What a small part after all in these calculations is corporeal man ; but spirit, that manifests through mind, is eternal, and knows not time, for it has no age. Man’s physical body is a part of nature. He comes under the same laws of Birth and Death, of Fate and Destiny. This is what the horoscope reveals to one—what these laws are to which we respond in nature. It is only in Spirit we are free, and Spirit, as the poet Browning said, is “ Life, Truth and Love ”— the stream that rolls on for ever. As long

title: Introductory Meditations

text: these laws are to which we respond in nature. It is only in Spirit we are free, and Spirit, as the poet Browning said, is “ Life, Truth and Love ”— the stream that rolls on for ever. As long as one identifies’ oneself with the desires of the physical body, one will respond to the horoscope, being still the “ nature ” that is in one, and not Spirit ! The truth of this one realises when studying the horoscopes of those who have responded to their passions, often being led by them into violence— having acted according to their elements, not being able to rise out of their evil circle or radiation, through failing to develop the power of true spiritual perception. This is where religions are of immense help to humanity, for through prayer the sub-conscious mind can perceive these great spiritual forces, which the scheming mind of reason knows nothing about. It brings a nearer commune with the Creator—it is the dove that descends upon the waters of the soul. Kach day for a few minutes let go the mind of calculation, relax the conscious will, close the eyes, feeling oneself bathed in that ethereal fire of the Spirit—Divine Love. It is a marvellous blending with—Reality ! This is a potent spell that can make woman

title: Introductory Meditations

text: let go the mind of calculation, relax the conscious will, close the eyes, feeling oneself bathed in that ethereal fire of the Spirit—Divine Love. It is a marvellous blending with—Reality ! This is a potent spell that can make woman beautiful, and man strong and free from care. To know one’s horoscope is to realise the force or motion in nature that influences one, and the type of brain through which one expresses the mind. Just as there is a physical sun in the heavens, so there is a golden sun of intelligence in the imagination. In spirit we are free ; but nature, like man, has its laws, and if they appear more cruel, remorseless and exacting, it is because they are concerned with greater causes and effects. The horoscope shows these laws of nature or vibrations, and as most men and women respond to

title: Introductory Meditations
location: Introductory Meditations
text: these shrill notes—of the pipes of Pan, it is perhaps just as well to understand these forces. For man’s body is his home, built by the Sun, Moon, and Stars. Astrology was the first of all the sciences and the parent of Astronomy, but now it is an element that is renounced and rejected by its child. Astronomy knows only the external visible heavens, Astrology the internal invisible cosmos. This old occult science was believed in by the great philosophical minds of the past, and is as ancient as the hills; but as man’s mind got more material and arrogant he could only understand external effects, until Astrology became so neglected that remnants of this knowledge fell into the hands of charlatans who imposed on the credulous—wandering tribes and gipsies, who live close to nature, had still a smattering of it. But it is only within the last few years that educated minds have been again interested in it. . The Divine Spirit speaks to man through the light of nature ; for do not the elements in the mineral, vegetable, and animal kingdoms form man’s physical body ? It is this fundamental principle that makes Saturn in the cosmos act upon that element that constitutes Saturn in man. To be in harmony with nature’s laws, one must neither abuse

text: vegetable, and animal kingdoms form man’s physical body ? It is this fundamental principle that makes Saturn in the cosmos act upon that element that constitutes Saturn in man. To be in harmony with nature’s laws, one must neither abuse nor misuse them, for evil is but perverted good. Astrology gives one the knowledge to understand one’s relative position in this great scheme of nature, for all things are connected together by a common principle—an invisible fire—which we know as life. There is nothing evil in life itself, for life is spirit. Man has been given control over these forces in nature, through wisdom—that comes not from the earth, nor from the stars. The chart of the heavens as given at the birth into this terrestrial planet shows Nature’s signet that has been placed upon the physical body and the earthly pilgrimage; teaching one wisdom through nature, for all these forces in creation can be used for good or evil—<Astrology indicating the inclination as revealed by the horoscope. In this book are actual experiences of this old occult science, and not merely speculations. They are gathered from the lives of many people of different nationalities, religions, philosophies, and social positions. title: Seven Planets
location: Section I — Sub-section I — Seven Planets
text: Astrologically a “ House”’ corresponds with a sign of the Zodiac; that is, a certain place in the heavens divided by 30 degrees, which is sub-divided by decanates of 10 degrees. The houses of the horoscope are stationary like the figures on a clock; the signs move from east to west—like its hands, from left to right. The planets move in an opposite direction from 1 degree to 30 degrees in the signs; these signs are called the ‘‘ Houses”’ of the Planets. The meaning of this is, if the planets are in any of the signs we call their “ houses,”’ they are essentially strong—for the planets in their revolutions go through all the houses, being either in sympathy with or antipathy to each; for some they delight in more than others, and have their exaltations, which is a strong position for good ; also certain degrees of the signs which are given as their Terms—or decanates. Planets in their fall or detriment are weak in power. Accidental dignities are when a planet is swift in motion, angular, or in sextile aspect with Jupiter or Venus. From an old manuscript: “From the eldest time man has shaped out a chart of the illimitable heavens—which has a mysterious link with our hidden future ! “For are we not pilgrims from an eternal home to an unknown bourne ? ” The Rising Sign The sign that is rising at birth is known in Astrology as the Ascendant or Ist House. It is the eastern horizon where day begins and the Sun rises. At whatever time of the day or night a birth takes place, one of the twelve signs of the Zodiac will be rising in the east. The Ascendant, or 1st House of one’s horoscope is relative to the health and vitality—temperament, appearance, and manner of bearing, especially in the young years of the life ; it is in alliance with the Moon; this is before the beneficent Sun comes to his full glory and strength in this map of the heavens. text: Cardinal, Fire. Aries. Ruler, Mars 3 (This is a hot and fiery sign.) Those born under this sign have indomitable courage and physical energy, enterprise and daring, that ceaseless activity which those born under signs not so robust find so difficult to comprehend. They are enthusiastic leaders and pioneers, seeking responsibility and never so happy as when they have many interests depending upon their abilities. This sign gives great vitality and a strong constitution, with a body spare and wiry—large bones; . with red or sandy hair, light eyes, blue or grey, with a quick bright look. It governs the head and face. When this sign is ill-dignified and the ruler afflicted, those born under it meddle in every affair, always making a muddle of what they choose to undertake. They are quick to give advice. Being restless and unquiet, they dabble in many things, but bring nothing to success. They are sometimes fanatical, which is seen in their excitable-looking eyes, and they often have a violent temper. This sign rules over the colour white, and its stones are the diamond and crystal. text: This sign governs the shoulders and arms. Its colours are white and crimson. Its stone, red carnelian. Cardinal, Water. Cancer a. Ruler, The Moon » (By nature cold and moist.) It gives great love of travellmg—especially long journeys by sea. Where those born under it have a great ideal, they will be constant and persevering, otherwise they are subject to moods. They are affected by environment, and readily respond to those who show them understandmg— as they are very sensitive. They are inclined to cling to the old traditions, and will keep all through their lives gifts of sentimental value. The Moon, the ruler of this sign, badly aspected makes them seekers of sensation—ruled by their emotions and sentiments. Both the good and bad aspects bring love of the domestic life. They are often too indolent to be bad tempered, their tempers would rather take the form of neglect. The constitution is not strong; the stomach and digestive organs are ruled by this sign. The diet must under certain aspects of the planets be studied, if they are to develop into strong men and women. The colours of the sign are russet brown and green. The aquamarine is its stone. text: Fixed, Air. Aquarius x. Ruler, Saturn h (It is an airy and masculine sign.) Although it can give a sharp penetrating character, it also confers idealism, with love of art and beauty. Those born under this sign are naturally experimentalists and investigators, preferring studies and interests which take them away from the beaten track of everyday thought. They seek solitude, for it is necessary for them— as they are liable to be drawn by the world—and they intuitively feel the healing power of nature. They carry out their work with patience and calmness, they are sympathetic and interested in human nature, keeping an unbiassed mind, and in their views ingenious and candid. Sometimes they are a little uncertain in their decisions. The weaker type are eccentric in their ideas with weird and fantastic inclinations. There are more blue-eyed people born under this sign than any of the other signs of the Zodiac. Fresh air is essential to them if they are to keep well and strong, especially in their youth. This sign rules the blood circulation, and nerves. Its colours are sky-colour or blue. The stones, the sapphire and jade. Mutable, Water. Pisces *. Ruler Jupiter 21 (It is a watery, cold, and feminine sign.) The highest we can give is service, and this is the meaning of this sign.

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — The Sun And The Moon
text: The Moon ) Tuts is the earth’s satellite. She is the nearest to the earth of all the seven planets. She passes through the twelve signs of the Zodiac in a little less than twenty-eight days— remaining two and a half days im each sign. The Moon and the earth have a very magnetic influence over one another, and science tells us ‘“‘ not only does the Sun pull the planets—but the Sun and Moon pull all portions of the earth,” and that all things animate and inanimate sympathise and vary with her; for man is one with this universal force, the brain being in sympathy with the elements of the Moon. The Moon is greatly influenced by the characteristics of the sign she is passing through—as she is negative, magnetic, moist and fruitful, and cold by nature. The temperament is much influenced by the Moon, for the Moon shows the inherited disposition —instincts, inclinations, and desires, the fundamental and natural side of one’s character. The characteristics of the Moon in the twelve signs of the Zodiac are given in the following subsection. The Sun or the ruler Mars in good aspect to the Moon gives prominent and successful careers, conferrng a quick mind with much self-confidence and an active nature—with the capabilities of bemg a leader or the holder of a responsible position. The bad aspects of these planets are inclined to make one too impulsive—restless, with lack of mental concentration, and sometimes fits of angry temper. In a man’s horoscope they bring inconstancy to the other sex. The “ luminous sphere ” that radiates around one, and is called by modern occultists “the Aura,” takes the colour of the sign the Moon is in at birth— especially in the young years. In this sign it is tinged with flame colour of so pale a hue that at times it is almost white. text: Lhe Moon }) in Scorpio N Making the nature passionate, but well aspected by the benefics—the Sun, Jupiter, and Venus—it gives great self-control, self-confidence, constancy. The possessors of such natures are also elusive with an air of mystery—being self-contained; giving few their confidences. This is a difficult sign to understand, for these people can be so passionately devoted, but immovable when they go to the other extreme. Under bad aspects they are stubborn, rude, and callous. They must guard against wrong impulses, such as would bring them remorse and regret. They rarely see from any other pomt of view than their own; and are very emphatic in their manner of speaking. The “luminous sphere” radiates with many shades of reds and pinks—these lights are often rare and uncommon, but intense and bright. In the case of the type of scorpio who are exacting, selfish, and cruel—then a muddy brown atmosphere obscures the radiance as clouds veil the sun. title: The Sun And The Moon At
location: Section II — Sub-section IV — The Sun And The Moon At
text: The Sun © It is a commonplace that the earth is small and the Sun and its flames are vast. The Solar god of Thebes—Ammon-Ra—shed his rays over Egypt about five thousand years before our era !

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section IV — The Sun And The Moon
text: The Ancient Egyptians, through the buried senses of the soul, were conscious of the hidden forces of the Universe, and the all-powerful emanation of the influence of the Sun on the life of man ; so to this great force they gave all reverence and honour, and worshipped this spirit of the Sun in his majesty and power. In their training of the body —and in all knowledge gained in learning—they did not separate the mind and spirit, as do our modern scientists ; for the present-day method of education seems now to leave unsatisfied the needs of one’s higher intelligence, and of the soul—the sojourner in its earthly home. Astrology is a study of a part of Nature in its relation to man, being a link with the Universe. The Planets, including the Harth, receive their light from the Sun, being “his family”; but not so the fixed stars, which are suns themselves—sometimes three to four times brighter and millions of times bigger. The solar vibrations penetrate our bodies, as rain does the earth. The Sun is the life emanation and is of such a magnetic and electric nature that it attracts or repels sympathetic or antipathetic forces through the sign it is in at birth. The Sun’s influences at the nativity will develop as character, built up by

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section IV — The Sun And The Moon
text: emanation and is of such a magnetic and electric nature that it attracts or repels sympathetic or antipathetic forces through the sign it is in at birth. The Sun’s influences at the nativity will develop as character, built up by environment, associations, education, and thought; for as a man thinks, that is what he will become. The Sun in the horoscope shows the mner centre of one’s being, it is the spiritual will—the “ I am.” Paracelsus says: “‘ He who only knows the outer man—knows an illusion.” The month of the year in which birth takes place is the sign of the Zodiac in which the Sun is, showing the cosmic elements attracted through that sign. The Sun never varies its signs from year to year. Just as sun-storms bring disturbances of the earth’s magnetism—so the cross aspects—squares, opposition, and sometimes conjunction of the Sun with the Moon and planets—will bring “soul storms,” showing as temper, passion, jealousy, envy, fear, malignity, and cruelty and moods of deep depression—so disturbing are these invisible forces. But an exalted nature caused by a desire for the good lifts up the soul! These are the Sun aspects of trine, sextile, and conjunction with the benefics:—Jupiter, wisdom; Venus, compassion and love ; Mercury, the higher intelligence ; with the trine or sextile of Mars,

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section IV — The Sun And The Moon
text: a desire for the good lifts up the soul! These are the Sun aspects of trine, sextile, and conjunction with the benefics:—Jupiter, wisdom; Venus, compassion and love ; Mercury, the higher intelligence ; with the trine or sextile of Mars, justice and courage ; Saturn, patience, faithfulness, endurance; Uranus, responsibility, power rightly used; Neptune, intuition, spiritual grace. The Sun does not come to his full power in a horoscope until the age of 30-35 years—for some develop younger than others; when once his power manifests, it is the dominant factor—until the end of the mortal life—for good or ill.

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section IV — The Sun And The Moon
text: The Sun passes through the twelve signs of the Zodiac in a year. Ptolemy says: ‘ His angel is Michael.” All the planets are his friends, but Saturn, Mars, Uranus, and Neptune are the least sympathetic to him. His orb is 12 degrees—applying or separating— the nearer his aspect, the more powerful the effect ; his influence weakens after passing the 7th degree. His metal is gold, and his stones the topaz, amber, chrysolite, and all yellow stones. The colours he rules over are—orange, yellow, and all golden shades. The Sun changes his sign about the 21st to the 23rd of the month—allow 7 degrees in a sign before he can express fully that part of the Zodiac. The Sun in Aries—March 22nd to April 20th. Ruler, Mars 3 The “native” is hopeful—self-reliant, for here the Sun gives energy and a liking for an active life ; there is no shirking responsibilities, but a firm will, courage in action, and a desire to lead others. Here are the pioneers and rulers—they give of the best and their all, having that enthusiasm of spirit which seems to draw the vitality that is necessary for their enterprises. They have naturally healthy constitutions. There is a fearlessness, force, and virility about this sign that make it the symbol of the Anglo-Saxon races. But

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section IV — The Sun And The Moon
text: enthusiasm of spirit which seems to draw the vitality that is necessary for their enterprises. They have naturally healthy constitutions. There is a fearlessness, force, and virility about this sign that make it the symbol of the Anglo-Saxon races. But when the Sun and the ruler of this sign, Mars, are in opposition or square to each other—or the Moon, Saturn, or Uranus are square or in opposition to the Sun or Mars—it shows as irritability and restlessness, with wrong impulses, and strong wayward passions, giving to the eyes a look of excitability with restless movements. It rules over the illnesses to the head, brain, mouth, and eyes—bringing danger of accidents to the head. The Sun in Taurus—April 21st to May 21st. Ruler, Venus 2 A practical nature allied with a keen artistic sense. There is power of mental endurance—determination and tenacity of purpose; a good imagination for writing, acting, and business. | Creative abilities in music, art, and dramatic work. On the practical side an aptitude for banking and management in business. There is always present a love of luxury and comfort. A square or opposition of the Moon, Mars, or Saturn to the Sun or Venus will make the nature sensuous, jealous, opinionated, and conceited, with a strong desire for material pleasures, and much attached to money and

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section IV — The Sun And The Moon
text: luxury and comfort. A square or opposition of the Moon, Mars, or Saturn to the Sun or Venus will make the nature sensuous, jealous, opinionated, and conceited, with a strong desire for material pleasures, and much attached to money and worldly possessions. In illness it affects the neck and throat.

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section IV — The Sun And The Moon
text: The Sun in Gemini—May 22nd to June 21st. Ruler, Mercury Gives a brilliant rather than a deep intellect. There is great charm of manner—social adaptability. These people make themselves agreeable to all classes by their courteous kindness. They are witty and clever speakers ; with talent for science, law, and literature. This is a double sign and hence gives interest in two things at a time. There is love of travel—and changes are necessary, there bemg a dislike to living in a groove. Uranus or Mars in square or opposition to the Sun or Mercury—under these bad aspects we find superficiality with a tendency to take up many things and finish few. Saturn square with or in opposition to the Sun in this sign makes the nature “ crafty,’ deceitful, and intriguing. When these bad aspects are to the ruler, Mercury, effort should be made to concentrate the mind with a view to overcoming the changeability and restlessness of this dualistic sign. It rules over the lungs and nervous system. The Sun in Cancer—June 22nd to July 23rd. Ruler, the Moon > The Sun in this sign brings more long journeys by sea than any of the other twelve signs of the Zodiac. It gives always a great desire to travel. A clever, imaginative, receptive mind with a tenacious memory.

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section IV — The Sun And The Moon
text: Moon > The Sun in this sign brings more long journeys by sea than any of the other twelve signs of the Zodiac. It gives always a great desire to travel. A clever, imaginative, receptive mind with a tenacious memory. They make good travellers, and others find them easy to get on with, as they are soon in harmony with their surroundings—being sympathetic and agreeable in manner. The opposition or square of the Sun and Moon gives timidity and very little self-reliance. Saturn Square or in opposition to the Sun or Moon here is miserliness and a self-centred disposition. Family traditions and the past seem to play a great part in their thoughts and conversation. Unless the Sun is well fortified by trmes and sextiles of the Moon and planets the health will be far from strong, and there will be a tendency to weakness of the digestive organs and stomach troubles. To be happy those under this sign require harmony in their domestic relations ; for devotion to the home life is one of their chief characteristics. The Sun in Leo—July 24th to August 23rd. Ruler, the Sun © Underneath the pride and austerity of manner that this sign so often gives we find noble, faithful characters. The trine or sextile of Jupiter or Saturn makes them strong-willed, but

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section IV — The Sun And The Moon
text: in Leo—July 24th to August 23rd. Ruler, the Sun © Underneath the pride and austerity of manner that this sign so often gives we find noble, faithful characters. The trine or sextile of Jupiter or Saturn makes them strong-willed, but humane in disposition. The Moon also in aspect gives warm sympathies, and having a sense of their responsibilities in the circle of their influence. The trine or sextile of Mars gives passion and force to the nature, but there is nothing mean or

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section IV — The Sun And The Moon
text: petty about them; they can be very indignant in © temper, but always magnanimous. The Sun afflicted by the square or opposition of the Moon seems to make them headstrong, passionate, and reckless. Uranus, also in bad aspect, produces very difficult characters, often using their power and authority with a high hand, assuming and domineering. Neptune, square, opposition, or conjunction, is a most unfortunate aspect to the Sun in this sign; it brings the counterfeit in love—especially if Venus is also afflicted—trusting those who scheme and intrigue for their own advantage. In health this sign rules the heart and spine. The Sun in Virgo—August 24th to Sept. 23rd. Ruler, Mercury % A quick, cheerful and alert disposition—a versatile mind, with love of method and order in work, good at business, but preferring literature; also inclined to the study of medicine and hygiene. There is an appreciation of everything clean, nice, and pure—rather than grandeur or ostentation. They are always critical and so a little difficult to please. The opposition or square of the Moon, Saturn or Neptune, gives lack of self-confidence and the right kind of pride—associating with those of humbler position and mental attainments, and so becoming doubting and selfish. Mercury, the ruler of this sign, trine or sextile, the Moon in conjunction with the Sun and rising, give

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section IV — The Sun And The Moon
text: self-confidence and the right kind of pride—associating with those of humbler position and mental attainments, and so becoming doubting and selfish. Mercury, the ruler of this sign, trine or sextile, the Moon in conjunction with the Sun and rising, give great abilities and make a bright and vivacious speaker with a philosophical mind. Education is not thrown away on this type of brain, as they have the faculty for absorbmg knowledge and should have a classical education. The sign rules over the bowels and intestines. The Sun in Inbra—Sept. 24th to Oct. 23rd. Ruler, Venus 2 It gives a love of justice and straightness, but a great dislike of unpleasantnesses. The nature is harmonious, with that sense of proportion that gives balance to the outlook on life and to the artistic perceptions. Many artists and musicians are born in this sign, for it gives musical rhythm and love of beauty and colour, and even if some form of art is not followed as a career, it makes the temperament sympathetic to the arts. Mars in opposition to or square with the ruler, Venus, or the Sun, gives great extravagance and would incline one to be influenced by desires and impulses. The Moon trine or sextile brmgs money, popularity, and that success which brings acknowledgment in public life. Much rest and

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section IV — The Sun And The Moon
text: the ruler, Venus, or the Sun, gives great extravagance and would incline one to be influenced by desires and impulses. The Moon trine or sextile brmgs money, popularity, and that success which brings acknowledgment in public life. Much rest and peace are required—more than under any other sign of the Zodiac—also the right companions, as it is the 7th sign, and so the house of union; feeling, loneliness, and craving for friendship and love. The square

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section IV — The Sun And The Moon
text: or opposition of Uranus, Neptune, and Saturn will bring an inclination to over-indulgence, laziness, sensuality, and indifference. They often suffer in health from illnesses of the kidneys and lower parts of the body. The Sun in Scorpio—Oct. 24th to Nov. 23rd. Ruler, Mars 3 This gives a shrewd, penetrating and sarcastic power of observation and detection, extreme in hatred as in love, but with the power of control against yielding to its impulse. Stern and unrelenting in temper. The “natives” admit into the life but few passions—these are tenacious and intense ; conscious that none who come into the daily life will sympathise with their deeper feelings—they veil them. Saturn or Neptune square or in opposition to the Sun or the ruler Mars clouds the heart and gives a coldness of mind—irony, an affected sneer; it often gives love of living in retirement—feeling alone in the world; in temper sullen and haughty. The trine or sextile of Mars or Uranus to the Sun, especially if Jupiter is in benefic aspect in the horoscope or elevated, gives a dignified and calm manner. Here are the natures that can give passionate devotion and faithfulness even to death, either to a cause or person loved, but they are silent and undemonstrative—often drawn to occultism or mysticism. With good aspects of Neptune it brings

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section IV — The Sun And The Moon
text: manner. Here are the natures that can give passionate devotion and faithfulness even to death, either to a cause or person loved, but they are silent and undemonstrative—often drawn to occultism or mysticism. With good aspects of Neptune it brings interest and communion between the living and the dead—that which is, and that which does not know mortality. In health, this sign rules over the generative system and bladder. The Sun in Sagittarius—Nov. 24th to Dec. 22nd. Ruler, Jupiter 21. Here are the philosophers—attracted by learning and culture. When the ruler Jupiter is trine or sextile with the Sun they have an easy path through life, bemg cheerful, hopeful, with that happy spontaneity that brings many friends. They are fond of the social amenities; making charming hosts and hostesses, having a free and generous disposition, with that lightness of manner in conversation and dealing with others that makes for success in the world—giving out a friendly welcome to all who come within their circle of influence. The trine and sextile of the Moon, Mars or Uranus, will bring love of travel, country life and sport, and kindness to animals. Saturn, Uranus, or Mars square or in opposition to the Sun or ruler Jupiter, gives fits of angry temper and jealousy, restlessness, prodigality—self-centred and bigoted; also when the Moon is square

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section IV — The Sun And The Moon
text: travel, country life and sport, and kindness to animals. Saturn, Uranus, or Mars square or in opposition to the Sun or ruler Jupiter, gives fits of angry temper and jealousy, restlessness, prodigality—self-centred and bigoted; also when the Moon is square or in opposition they will try to force their own point of view on others. In health the sign rules the thighs and hips.

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section IV — The Sun And The Moon
text: The Sun wm Capricornus—Dec. 23rd to Jan. 20th. Ruler, Saturn h The natures thus influenced are practical and deliberate—loving forms and ceremonies, orderly and obedient. It gives quickness and subtility, making clever diplomats and politicians—often having witty minds. It is only to the few they give their confidences, as they rarely disclose their plans to others, always keeping a great reserve. Much power of mental concentration to shape their ideas into form. When the Sun and ruler of this sign, Saturn, are trine or sextile it gives calmness in danger, with a discriminating character, reserve and caution; if the Moon is also in good aspect, it makes them just and good-humoured. The square or opposition of Saturn or the Moon expresses itself in discontent, repinmg and melancholy. This sign in health rules over the knees, bones and skin. The Sun in Aquarius—Jan. 21st to Feb. 19th. Rulers, Saturn h and Uranus # Here we see idealists, but with an understanding of human nature; original in their ideas, with a great love for their fellow-man. They are tactful and prudent—for they are clever judges of character ; obliging and taking an interest in their friends, being attached to their social circle. It is necessary for them to be alone for a given time each day, to recuperate their strength—for they give

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section IV — The Sun And The Moon
text: are clever judges of character ; obliging and taking an interest in their friends, being attached to their social circle. It is necessary for them to be alone for a given time each day, to recuperate their strength—for they give out so much vitality through their sympathies. Taking broad ideas of life, they are often misunderstood, by those who are more conventional or live in prescribed circles. These are the natures that are more often drawn to great spiritual conceptions than religious forms or ceremonies. Scientific investigations interest them, and all that makes for progress on new or unexplored lines. The conjunction, square, or opposition of Saturn, Uranus, or Neptune will incline them to be very eccentric, unpractical and unreliable, especially if the Moon also is in evil aspect; thus their word is not to be relied upon. They often suffer from moods of deep depression, especially under the transits of Uranus, Saturn, and Neptune. In health the sign rules the blood and the nerves. The Sun in Pisces—Feb. 20th to March 21st. Rulers, Neptune Y and Jupiter 21 This gives a nature loving, kind and compassionate, always ready to help. There is not a great deal of vitality or a strong will, without Jupiter, Uranus, or Mars be trine or sextile to the Sun; or Jupiter m conjunction. Artistic

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section IV — The Sun And The Moon
text: gives a nature loving, kind and compassionate, always ready to help. There is not a great deal of vitality or a strong will, without Jupiter, Uranus, or Mars be trine or sextile to the Sun; or Jupiter m conjunction. Artistic poetical temperaments ; if the Sun is in conjunction with Venus and Mercury, especially with

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section IV — The Sun And The Moon
text: a benefic aspect of the Moon, it will give a highly imaginative mind, with histrionic talents. The Square or opposition of Saturn, Neptune, or the Moon inclines them to be indolent and phlegmatic, and then there is often difficulty about self-expression. To the appearance it gives a tendency to stooping shoulders, and often fatness of the body. When Mars or Uranus is square, in opposition, or conjunction to the Sun in this sign, it gives a passionate temper, extravagance, and prodigality. In health this sign rules over the lungs, nerves and feet.

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section V — The Sun And The Moon
text: THE Moon’s PowEr The Moon materialises what the Sun and planets promise in the horoscope. Well dignified on an angular house, with a trine or sextile of the Sun or a trine-sextile or conjunction Jupiter or Venus, the pathway of life is brilliant, happy, and successful, for under these aspects the Moon brings the good things of this world. Her great enemy amongst the planets is Mars, and when in square, opposition, or conjunction it has a bad effect on the health and bram. Saturn when in square, opposition, or conjunction is also injurious for health, especially during the first seven years of life, and in after years with bad aspects or transits it again brings misfortune, for it has a depressing effect, leading one to a cul-de-sac in the career and affairs. Her angel is Gabriel, and her friends among the planets are the Sun, Jupiter, Venus, Mercury, sometimes Neptune; but Saturn, Mars, and Uranus are the least sympathetic to her. Her colours are greenish-blue mixed with white. Her metal is silver, and her stones are pearls, opals, crystals, and diamonds. The Moon rules the left side and the bladder, all cold and rheumatic diseases, feminine weaknesses. When she is ill-aspected at birth it makes the person temperamentally lazy, especially the square or opposition of Venus, also bad aspects

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section V — The Sun And The Moon
text: and diamonds. The Moon rules the left side and the bladder, all cold and rheumatic diseases, feminine weaknesses. When she is ill-aspected at birth it makes the person temperamentally lazy, especially the square or opposition of Venus, also bad aspects of Neptune make one very untidy. If Mars adds an evil aspect, there is a tendency to drmk; the square or opposition with Mercury, untruthfulness. But when the Moon is in trine, sextile or conjunction with Mercury it strengthens the mind, giving mental energy, being the best and strongest aspect for a clever intellect. With Venus also in aspect there is great talent for music and the fine Arts. The Moon in cardinal signs—Aries, Cancer, Libra, Capricornus—leads one out on to the great high-road of life, but unless she is fortified with strong benevolent aspects of the Sun or planets there will be danger of misfortune, as one will lack continuity and strength of purpose. A trine or sextile of Saturn here is of great help, giving caution and discrimination. } In fixed signs—Taurus, Leo, Scorpio, Aquarius— the Moon inclines the person to be patient, prudent,

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section V — The Sun And The Moon
text: just, constant, enduring; sometimes obstinate, stubborn, avaricious, and perversely resolute. In bicorporal or double signs—Gemini, Sagittarius, Pisces—will make the person impressionable, changeable, wavering, adaptable, careless, fond of music and the arts, but inclined to duplicity. In the mutable sign Virgo, ingenious but inconstant. This old occult science of Astrology is correlative and synthetic, so do not judge from one aspect or sign alone, but make your final decision from a blending of the whole of the horoscope. The signs and planets, especially the Sun and Moon if on the Cardinal Cross—that is the Ist, 10th, 7th, 4th houses —will be first in power (these are the sensitive points) in both the natal and progressed maps. Then the Sun and Moon are most important amongst the planets in whichever “ houses ” they are posited. Next in interest is the ruler of the horoscope— that is, the planet that is the ruler of the sign on the Ist house—the Asc. or planets in the Ist house, for a planet rising is one coming into power. The orb of the Moon is 12 degrees before and after any aspect. The closer the aspect, the stronger her power; her influence weakens after 7 degrees. For journeys, whether by sea or land, the Moon is the general significatrix.

title: The Cardinal Cross
location: Section II — Sub-section VI — The Cardinal Cross
text: The four most important “ Houses” of the horoscope are the Ist, 10th, 7th, and 4th. They form the Cardinal Cross. Whichever signs are on these houses will be the Cardinal Cross of the horoscope. It shows the difficulties, sorrows, experiences, joys, happiness, and successes that will be met with, to be stored and garnered in the sub-conscious mind ; for it is “the cross”’ that one must carry through this earthly pilgrimage. If one looks up at the heavens, turning the face to the Hast, for that is the first point of the Cardinal Cross—it is also the rising sign at one’s birth,—at the back will be the West—known in Astrology as the “descendant ” or “7th House.” Then directly over one’s head is the “10th House,” called the M.C.; at the feet is the 4th House, the I.C. or nadir. The Ist House is East, 10th South, 7th West, and the 4th House North—this is the magnetic North Pole of the earth. Malefics posited on any of these “ Houses” at birth or by progression will bring great experiences, which will chastise the soul, pointing to the lesson it has to learn—the burden it has to carry. Benefics will bring “ the gifts of the gods,” but

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section VI — The Sun And The Moon
text: they do not indicate so strong a character; for a child is not asked to carry the burdens of the man. An old writer on this subject says: “‘ Humanity has a vague craving for something that the earth cannot give, and a vague sense of a former existence in which that something was enjoyed.” This study of Astrology seems to be the lost key of those former states, but it is not a language of omen and doom, but of experience, for only by contrast can we know anything.

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section VII — The Sun And The Moon
text: THE Moon ON THE CARDINAL Cross lst House The Moon on this point of the horoscope signifies an inclination to change and inconstancy—having a sensitive intuitive mind, with gentle manners, loving pleasure and ease. In appearance, of middle height, with a round head, and full face, very pale skin, light large eyes, and the hair of that colourless fairness that the French call ‘“ blonde cendré,” with light eyebrows joming over the nose—which is short with the tip rounded. The whole body inclined to be fat. The square or opposition of Saturn brings timidity and changeability with reversals of fortune, and with an opposition of Mars and Mercury—in a close and direct ray—it brings danger of madness. The trine or sextile of the Sun in cardinal signs will bring many journeys. Uranus in aspect they come unexpectedly—the trine, sextile, or conjunction Venus or Jupiter. The “native”? meets with the help of powerful friends in the different countries he goes to, for in manner it gives understanding, with a gentle disposition that brings social success, making one fortunate in association with all classes. The Moon in the 10th House It gives a desire for public life and in conjunction, trine or sextile Jupiter, Venus, the Sun, or Uranus— brings the help and support of those in power, honours and popularity. Cardinal

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section VII — The Sun And The Moon
text: all classes. The Moon in the 10th House It gives a desire for public life and in conjunction, trine or sextile Jupiter, Venus, the Sun, or Uranus— brings the help and support of those in power, honours and popularity. Cardinal signs, changes of occupation; the mutable signs, long voyages and journeys. It is a critical position if the Moon is afflicted with a square, opposition, or conjunction Mars; it will bring scandals. Uranus, accidents and great losses. Saturn would impede the career in every way, bringing loss of credit and reputation. Neptune, no continuity, but changes and misfortunes. The Moon in the 7th House The trme or sextile of Uranus or the Sun will bring public success and fame. A happy, blissful marriage when the Moon is trine, sextile or conjunction Venus or Jupiter. Aspects of Mars and Mercury bring many changes —sometimes of country if in cardinal or mutable signs.

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section VII — The Sun And The Moon
text: The evil aspects of Saturn when square or opposition will bring public enmity, and if Uranus and Mars are in bad aspect, criticism; also unhappy relations with the other sex. Bringing mharmony, and under the bad transits, death of the marriage partner. Sometimes instability of fortune. The Moon in the 4th House This is the magnetic North Pole of the earth. With good aspects it is a strong position for happiness late in life; 1¢ gives interest in land and agriculture—a successful old age. The bad aspects, especially of Uranus, bring many changes of residence, unexpected and not always advantageous, for Uranus in evil aspect brings upheavals of a calamitous nature ; the parents meet with reversals of fortune. Neptune in square or opposition means sudden death of the mother. The malign aspects of the malefics to the Moon in this angle of the horoscope indicate family bereavements, estrangements and loss of inheritance, Saturn in this respect being more evil than any of the other planets. This is a critical position for the Moon—when afflicted by malefics—bringing grief and sorrow and often loneliness into the home life. 11th House——The Moon in the house of friends in fixed signs trine, sextile or conjunction Jupiter. —Venus, Mercury, or in good aspect to the Sun, will bring loving, faithful, intellectual and powerful friends.

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section VII — The Sun And The Moon
text: and often loneliness into the home life. 11th House——The Moon in the house of friends in fixed signs trine, sextile or conjunction Jupiter. —Venus, Mercury, or in good aspect to the Sun, will bring loving, faithful, intellectual and powerful friends. The square or opposition of Uranus, Neptune, Saturn, or Mars—-trouble and unhappiness through friendships. 9th House.—With aspects of Mars, Uranus or Mercury there will be journeys to far-off countries —the trine or sextile of the Sun, Saturn or Jupiter, great success in a foreign country; trine, sextile, or conjunction Venus, money from relations by marriage. Aspects of Neptune—the mind is visionary, there are strange dreams. 2nd House.—Ill-aspected by the square or opposition of the Sun, Jupiter or Saturn, great difficulties in monetary affairs. 5th House.—The square or opposition of the Sun or Venus—the children grow up unsympathetic to the parent. The trine, sextile or conjunction of Venus or Jupiter in the signs Cancer, Scorpio, Pisces will give many children who are beautiful, talented, and a joy. The trine or sextile of the Sun, Uranus or Mercury indicates success and money as a dramatist, actor, impresario, or organiser of entertamments for the younger generation. 3rd House.—Jupiter or Mercury trine, sextile or in conjunction the Moon gives a clever mind fond of learning, naturally attracted to the law

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section VII — The Sun And The Moon
text: and politics; music when trine, sextile or conjunction Venus; Mars or Uranus in good aspect will give mental energy—especially in airy signs; to the Sun—just, generous, high-minded. 6th House.—Aspects of Mars, Saturn, and the Sun, Square or opposition, brmg bad health and discomfort into: the home life; but the trine or sextile of Venus or Mercury would bring domestic comfort and good servants. 8th House.—lf afflicted by the square or opposition of the Sun, Saturn, Uranus, or Mars, there is danger of surgical operations or serious accidents. Every care should be taken when Mars transits these planets or this “‘ house ’’— passing once in two years. ) 12th House.—The Moon in this “ house’ often puts the early life into a backwater ; the evil aspects of Uranus, Saturn or Mars, produce many enemies. Cardinal or mutable signs—long journeys. Dignified by trine or sextile of the Sun, Jupiter or Mercury; a clever unobtrusive mind, with great success later in life. Sus-section VIII

title: The Sun On The Cardinal Cross
location: Section II — Sub-section VII — The Sun On The Cardinal Cross
text: lst House For the Sun to have this position in the horoscope shows that one was born in the morning, about 8 to 9am. It is the Asc. Airy or fiery signs on this house are favourable, for if the Sun is well aspected by trines or sextiles of the Moon or planets, one would be honourable, faithful, intuitive, just, and courteous. It gives to the appearance regular features, a fine skin with a soft rosy colour in the cheeks and lips, with the most beautiful red-gold hair, the eyes of a golden-brown or greenish hazel; but if the birth should take place during an eclipse, it gives weak sight and sometimes a slight cast in the eyes. The eyelashes and eyebrows a little darker than the hair ; the forehead is rounded and prominent without being high ; the bones of the face are not at all apparent ; the cheeks inclined to be plump. To the fortunes it gives long life, honour and success, dignity of manner, with much self-reliance. A trine or sextile of Jupiter or Mars gives a strong body with much vitality. But when the Sun is afflicted by a square or opposition of Uranus, Mars, or Saturn it brings restlessness—a spendthrift nature, boastful and without judgment.

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section VII — The Sun And The Moon
text: The Sun in the 10th House The Sun in this house of the horoscope—called the M.C.—would show that one was born about midday. Itis with good aspects of Jupiter and the Moon the most favourable hour out of the twenty-four. It seems to give a charmed life—bestowmg every gift, honour, eminence, great successes ; for it overcomes all difficulties and obstacles—and after the 30th year brings power, so that the credit cannot be assailed. The square or opposition of Saturn, Uranus, or Mars would make one too self-assertive, arrogant and proud ; of the Moon, extravagant. The Sun in the 7th House About six in the evening the Sun is passing through this house; so it would indicate that one was born about that time. This is a strong and important position for the Sun. It brings public recognition and fame attained by the career; especially with a trine or sextile of the Moon, Jupiter or Mars. In the woman’s horoscope—it foretells a brilliant marriage. With an opposition or square of Mars, Saturn or Uranus then bringing trouble and attacks from powerful people— to the career in public life ; also to the marriage, as these aspects bring great enmity and discord. The Sun in the 4th House This is birth near midnight; it makes the second half of the

title: The Sun And The Moon
location: Section II — Sub-section VII — The Sun And The Moon
text: from powerful people— to the career in public life ; also to the marriage, as these aspects bring great enmity and discord. The Sun in the 4th House This is birth near midnight; it makes the second half of the life most important and successful. The Moon trine or sextile the Sun will bring great success to the career at that time of the life, sometimes bringing benefits from the parents. The square or opposition of Saturn, Mars, Uranus, or Neptune will bring loss of inheritance and trouble through the father. A trine or sextile of Saturn, Uranus, or Neptune make one sensitive to psychic influences and interested in Astrology. The Sun in the 11th House-—The trine or sextile of the Moon or planets bring faithful friends of the nature of the planet in aspect: Saturn —older; Mercury—younger ; Venus—-women ; Mars—men. The Sun in conjunction Jupiter or Venus will bring great gain and extremely happy times through friends—the signs showing their position: Cardimal—well-known or famous people ; they also make one attracted to the social life. Fixed signs—unchanging friends. The mutable signs, one can get on well with most people and find friendly influences in all classes. The evil aspects of the square or opposition will bring trouble of the nature of the planet in bad aspect. 9th House.—The

title: The Sun And The Moon

title: Mercury
location: Section III — Sub-section IX — Mercury
text: PaRAcELsuS says: “The thoughts of great minds remain for ages like stars on the mental. horizon of the world.” The celerity of the mind can be likened to the rapidity of the movements of Mercury—which caused this planet to be known to the Ancients as the “ swift-winged messenger of the gods.”’ Its discovery is wrapped in mystery—and the record of it is lost in antiquity. “Hermes ” speaks of this sparkling little planet thousands of years before the Christian Era; and we read of an early account given by an astrologer to one of the Kings of Assyria—300 years B.c. The ancient Greeks called him “ The Sparkling One,” and named him after the “ Great Initiate Hermes,’ who is represented carrymg a wand around which are entwined two serpents—the rod of Mercury (caduceus), here the symbol of wisdom. He is armed with a short sword, and wears a cap with wings, and sandals; he is the patron of all travellers. He is the nearest known planet to the Sun—never getting further away than 28 degrees. Rising aS a morning star, with a conspicuous brightness in the absence of the Sun, his position is always near the horizon, and im the twilight of the sunset he scintillates with great power and brightness. . He shines with a ruddy lustre,

title: Mercury
location: Section III — Sub-section IX — Mercury
text: morning star, with a conspicuous brightness in the absence of the Sun, his position is always near the horizon, and im the twilight of the sunset he scintillates with great power and brightness. . He shines with a ruddy lustre, but is so involved in a dense atmosphere that at times he looks a silvery grey. He comes next to the Moon in rapidity of movement in the Zodiac—passing through the twelve signs in twelve months. In the course of a revolution he presents all the phases of the Moon—taking like her the influence of each sign he is in. In fiery signs he is alert, keen, and excitable. In watery signs, emotional and romantic ; but Scorpio, being a Mars sign, makes him changeable, giving a sarcastic expression to the thoughts. He expresses himself best in airy signs. Libra imdicates love of art and music ; Aquarius gives interest and liking for investigation in the realms of science; Gemini produces a clever penetrating intellect. In the earthy signs he is best in Virgo or Taurus. Capricornus inclines him to be selfish. He shines with a fitful lustre and is variable in nature, for he reflects the influences of the signs and aspects of the other planets, and expresses their meaning in thought—for he represents intellect.

title: Influence Of The Seven Planets
location: Section III — Sub-section IX — Influence Of The Seven Planets
text: The good aspects of the Moon bring beneficial changes, bestowing a clever mind and understanding. Aspecting many planets, there ensues an active and busy life. Ptolemy says: “‘ Mercury in one of the houses of Saturn, trine or sextile to that planet, gives excellent understanding. The Sun or Moon in conjunction with Mercury gives great intellectual abilities.” Another old astrologer says : ‘“‘ Mercury and Venus in conjunction in an airy sign in the Asc. will make one a great scholar and a learned critic. “‘ Mercury in an earthy sign in conjunction Saturn shows death by the bite of a mad dog, or the sting of some venomous creature. ‘Mercury trme or sextile the Moon gives the capacity for acquiring foreign languages. ‘Mercury in opposition or square the Moon, if the nature is unevolved, will incline it to be envious, sarcastic, ill-natured—given to lying and thieving.” Ptolemy says: “ His orb is 7 degrees before and after any aspect.” His friends among the planets are the Sun, Moon, Jupiter, Venus, Uranus and Neptune; but Mars in bad aspect is his worst enemy. The stones he rules over are all those of divers colours—white and red carnelians and marcasite or fire stone. His metal is quicksilver. Azure and all light blue shades are his colours. title: Venus
location: Section III — Sub-section X — Venus
text: “ Goddess of Beauty,” as the Ancients appropriately called her—and never was an honour more justly given. “Star of the Morning,” that heralds the rising glory of the Sun. “Star of the Evening,” lingering in his afterglow of the twilight—the radiant shining one, without a parallel im her splendour and beauty in the Solar system, for no object can compare with the silvery lustre of this planet Venus. She is the symbol in Astrology that gives to the race of man so much that is desirable, conferring beauty and bringing love into this earthly life; for she comes with poetry and music, those fabrications of genius and fairyland. Where Venus is posited in the horoscope will

Love is vitality—Life and Eternity. Death is—where love is not. All classes and races of mankind wish to be attractive and lovely to the one they adore, so that they can fascinate and inspire love in return—be it only a desire of the passions or a devotion of the soul !—for are not they different notes of the gamut of life ? God is love—all religions as given by the great Initiates bring a message of love, uplifting us with their compassion and mercy. There is no sadder experience than the parting with a mutual love; a little of us must die, for it brings not only loss of vitality but of the forces that are constructive ; for a great love of the mind or of the soul is a healing power for all ills to which the flesh is heir. Sacrifices made to this goddess are never lost, but like all great truths, she has her counterfeit, the proof of the counterpart. The counterfeit is relatively destructive—so it has no permanence. title: Jupiter
location: Section III — Sub-section XI — Jupiter
text: Shining with a strong, pale yellow light, coming second in brilliancy and much brighter than any of the fixed stars. He is the greatest planet in the Solar system; known as the “ All-night watcher of the heavens ” because for several months each year he is an allnight star. He remains one year in each sign of the Zodiac, passing through them all in twelve years. Orpheus, that divine enchanter with his sevenstringed lyre—which is represented as finally becoming a star, with each cord corresponding to one form of the human soul—was the high priest in the temples dedicated to Jupiter—thirteen centuries before the Christ. The ancient Greeks worshipped Jupiter as their God of righteousness and justice—the symbol of wisdom and the higher intelligence. In Astrology Jupiter comes next to the Sun in power, his influence giving eminence and riches; with trines and sextiles of the other planets, the Sun or Moon, he comes with both hands full—bringing joy, love, and beauty. He has a protective influence over the life and fortunes. Bemg very powerful when rising or on the M.C. —trine, conjunction or sextile the Sun, Moon, or Venus—giving years of good fortune, happiness, and success. All reigning monarchs have Jupiter prominent in their horoscopes; but ordinary mortals will find that he confers more benefits than any other

title: Jupiter
location: Section III — Sub-section XI — Jupiter
text: the M.C. —trine, conjunction or sextile the Sun, Moon, or Venus—giving years of good fortune, happiness, and success. All reigning monarchs have Jupiter prominent in their horoscopes; but ordinary mortals will find that he confers more benefits than any other planet when elevated or in trine or sextile and sometimes conjunction with the planets and the Sun or Moon. When in the 4th House—inherited titles and money. When rising trine, sextile or conjunction Mercury, he gives a sagacious mind, with intellectual abilities —resourceful with the strength of powerful arguments. A trine, sextile or conjunction Venus will make a faithful, disinterested friend. So few can resist the charm of this planet, Jupiter, for he gives gentleness of manner and a smile of kindness—being sincere, with the highest ideals for conduct and courtesy. There is no need to fear reversals with Jupiter holding a strong position in the horoscope. His enemies among the planets are Mars and Saturn. When they are in opposition or square aspect— especially from the Ist House—this will give an egotistical point of view—injuring the good fortune brought by Jupiter through faults of temperament. Mars in this position to Jupiter gives impulse about money, bringing loss and difficulties. If

Ptolemy says : ““ His orb is 9 degrees before and after any aspect. “ His angel is Zadkiel. “His stones are the emerald, amethyst, and sapphire. “ He rules over the colours red mixed with green. “ His metal is tin. “ His friends are all the planets’’—but Saturn, Mars, and Uranus are the least sympathetic to him. In the physical body he rules over the lungs and the blood—diseases and illnesses such as gout, pleurisy, and inflammation of the lungs. As a rule he gives robust health and a good constitution. He expresses himself best in the airy signs, Aquarius or Libra, or in his own signs, Pisces and Sagittarius, bestowing faithfulness, justice, generosity, even temper, poise and grace—bringing successful careers and sincere friends. The earthy and watery signs attract to social and public life—especially Jupiter in Cancer—and sport and entertaining. Jupiter is the planet of expansion, and brings those vibrations into one’s life; just as his enemy, Saturn, limits the mind and experiences, keeping one in a rut or groove. Jupiter has the power of helping one out of all difficulties and vexations. Jupiter on the Cardinal Cross lst House Jupiter

Sus-section XII Mars ¢ An eye like Mars to threaten and command.—SHAKESPEARE. Throughout the histories of the ages, Mars has always been the symbol for the “ God of War.” With the Ancient Greeks he is represented as a young, strong hero—wearing armour and a helmet, carrying a shield and spear—in a _ combative attitude, for he desires and searches after war—to attack, defend, and contend! That is why the other gods turn away from him—for he brings quarrels and discord. But the Roman Empire held him in high esteem for his power and genius in war—for they gave glory and honour to the strong, disgrace and shame to the weak. He is the patron of all valiant, industrious, and active natures, and of the tournaments and the chase. The trines and sextiles with the Sun and Moon in the horoscope will give extraordinary courage, for they shrmk from no danger, generous to a fault, daring in all things, loving war and all that belongs to it. If Mars is rising or on the M.C. with these aspects, it will give prowess and skill in all combats, contests and fights—and they know no peace except in victory.

text: Saturn was the most remote of all the planets known to the ancient writers on Astrology. That was in the days before the discovery of Uranus and Neptune. Saturn is represented in mythology as Chronos, an old man, in his right hand a scythe, im his left a serpent curled in a circle—symbol of time without beginning or end. His movement is slow—he takes nearly thirty years to pass through the twelve signs of the Zodiac ; a major planet, magnetic and masculine, but negative. This planet well aspected and dignified (that is in its exaltation Libra or its own houses Capricornus, Aquarius) with a trine or sextile of the Sun, Moon,

These benefic aspects of Saturn are of great value, giving a disciplined mind, without rashness or irritability. True and faithful as friends but naturally quiet and of few words, but as a rule correct in their judgment. The temperament of those born under the evil aspects of this cold, melancholy planet, principally the square or opposition of the Sun or Moon, will be suspicious and jealous, making them crafty and subtle; and if Mars also throws a bad aspect by square or opposition to the Sun, Moon, or Saturn, a murderous and malicious nature. Here are the people who commit the crimes of revenge ! The square or opposition of Mercury to Saturn inclines to discontent, envy, and untruthfulness. If Saturn be posited in the signs Libra, Sagittarius, Aquarius, Virgo, or well dignified by trines and sextiles of the Sun, Moon, Venus, or Mercury, the character will be expressed in a most favourable manner, as it endows one with a deep thinking mind —giving prudence and logical reasoning, skill in the management of one’s affairs, and a tactful manner, doing away with wrong impulses and carelessness. text: Uranus Known as Herschell’s planet, it takes 84 years to pass through all the signs of the Zodiac, remaining about seven years in a sign, as it moves from 4 to 5 degrees in a year. It is a major planet but most remote, variable in nature, uniting in its constitution the electric and magnetic conditions, which cause sudden disturbances and upheavals, as unexpected as a flash of lightning across a summer’s sky—‘“A bolt from the blue.” Like Neptune, its effect on mankind is outside the three-dimensional natural kingdom that rules over our physical senses. The mind of reason, of calculation, cannot deal or cope with it as it has a dislocating effect, bringing sudden unlooked for conditions and changes, both to the life and environment. It is the connecting principle between the third and fourth dimension. It has much influence over the luminous sphere —that is the magnetism that surrounds us—and all living creatures. Within it lies the power to attract or repel, and it can strongly affect the magnetism of others even at a distance. Some people quite unconsciously have the power of drawing this life force out of another person’s Aura; it happens more often in married life, or where there is a close companionship. The one will become so depleted as to go

When this planet is ill-aspected, it makes the person eccentric, often with foolish mannerisms, appearing to lose control over the movements of the physical body—wanting poise and balance. Dressing in the brightest colours and weird patterns, likmg the effect of sensations, the bad aspects, especially the square or opposition of the Sun, Moon, or Venus, produces odd people impossible to control, as they dislike advice and prefer to be bad originals. It has a devastating power when on the angles and in cardinal signs with cross aspects. Ruling, as it does, the magnetism of our influence under the evil aspects, it strongly repels others, bringing estrangements and misunderstandings, upsets and discord, when our nearest and dearest turn from us. These bad aspects bring more divorces and broken engagements than any other planet’s influence, for the loved one is cold, appearing indifferent, distrait, after being ardent and passionate, making one feel not wanted, which brings a sense of void and loss. These are the vibrations that so often cause suicide in unhappy love affairs, especially configurated with evil aspects of Saturn, for unity is strength in Astrology as elsewhere. title: Neptune Y
location: Section III — Sub-section XV — Neptune Y
text: This planet is the most remote in our Solar System. It takes about 156 years to pass through the twelve signs of the Zodiac, which the Moon does in a month.

title: Influence Of The Seven Planets
location: Section III — Sub-section XV — Influence Of The Seven Planets
text: Neptune has the slowest movement of any planet. Science tells us that each backward movement, that is when it is retrograding (this symbol, R, is always placed after a planet when it is m its backward course), is more than half as long as a forward movement. The planets all go round the Sun in the same direction, but this is not true of their rotation on their axes, for the two outer planets, Neptune and Uranus, spin round their axes in the reverse direction to the other planets. Neptune has one moon, that moves quite differently from any of the other satelites in relation to their planets. In the ordinary way it is very difficult to understand its strange vibrations, for it is really outside the three dimensional conditions of our physical senses—that is why it is so perplexing for the generality of mankind to respond in a normal way to itsinfluence. There is no doubt it is the planet of chaos, in the way it expresses itself in our thoughts and emotions, for all that is vague and mdefinite answers to its vibrations, yet its influence for good or evil is quite defined. It is the planet of laughter, and it also brings tears. The good aspects on the natal horoscope, progressed or by transit, will bring extraordinarily

It is a time when one’s heart is filled with that delicious, vague joy which the very excess of rapture alone produces. These are the loves of the soul. title: The Horoscope Or Map Of The Heavens
location: Section IV — The Horoscope Or Map Of The Heavens
text: Tus is simply a plan of the heavens, representing an accurate scheme of the position of the Sun, Moon, planets, and fixed stars at the time of birth, which should be known exact to within fifteen minutes. The map contains the twelve houses and signs of the Zodiac. The Zodiac is an imaginary band or belt, measuring about 18 degrees in breadth. The ecliptic, or path of the Sun, passes exactly through the centre of the Zodiac, longitudinally. It was divided by Ptolemy into twelve equal parts of 30 degrees each. This map of the heavens was sometimes drawn square, but the circular form is, perhaps, more easily understood and more generally used. It is formed by three circles. (See Diagram, p. 141.) The centre space is used for the date of birth. The next space is divided into twelve equal parts which form the houses of the horoscope—reserved for the ©, ), and planets. Then, in the outer space are placed the signs of the Zodiac, with their degrees on the cusp of each house. The line between two houses is the cusp. (See Diagram, p. 26.) You must now refer to the particular ephemeris, sometimes called an Astrological Almanack (Raphael’s and Zadkiel’s are both good), drawn up for the year of the birth. We will suppose one is

You must now refer to the particular ephemeris, sometimes called an Astrological Almanack (Raphael’s and Zadkiel’s are both good), drawn up for the year of the birth. We will suppose one is born at 8.30 a.m. on August 28th, 1884 ; an ephemeris for that year should be obtained. Under August 28th, on the left-hand column, “‘ Sidereal time ”’ is written---this is Greenwich time,* 12 o’clock mid-day. If the birth is before noon, from this “ Sidereal time ” the difference between the time of birth and noon of that day must be deducted. But if the birth happened after noon, then the difference between noon and the time given must be added to the “Sidereal time” of that day. If the ‘“Sidereal time” is small, as at the Vernal Equinox, March 21st, so that no deduction can be made, then twenty-four hours must first be added to the “ Sidereal time ’’ and the deduction then made. But suppose it was the Autumn Equinox, so that by adding the time to the “ Sidereal time,”’ the total exceeds the twenty-four hours; then the twentyfour hours must be deducted from the “ Sidereal time,’ the remainder used in the calculations. text: The ruler of the sign on the ascendant, planet or planets in the 1st House, is the ruler of the horoscope. If it is a benefic and elevated, it promises preferment and great honour, making the life interesting and successful. Ptolemy says: “If the ruler of the Ascendant or planet in the 1st House is a benefic, and the © and >) are in 6, so likewise if the luminaries are in 2, from the 1st to the 7th House, the person will be highly prosperous in all thmgs that befall him; but the contrary effect will be produced should a malefic be on the ascendant.”

The Nautical Almanack gives their right ascension and declination. In certain positions in the horoscope their power is undeniable, especially the conjunction and opposition; but the A, O, and *% aspects have no effect.
